title: [v4.1.1-rhel] Cirrus: Remove build-push tasks
body:
These tasks only ever run on the main branch.  For whatever reason, the
meta task is throwing errors trying to update a non-existant
`build-push-${IMAGE_SUFFIX}` image.  I have no idea how/why this image
got pruned, it shouldn't have.  However, it appears so far to be a
one-off, and it's not actually needed.  Remove reference to it and
the related useless tasks on this branch.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR

string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: @edsantiago @lsm5 PTAL (quick one).  I'm slightly bothered this image went *poof*, but it's only happened on this branch, and is otherwise inconsequential, so I'm ignoring it.  However, if this happens again elsewhere, please let me know and I'll investigate more thoroughly.

The string "multiarch" no longer appears in `.cirrus.yml`, nor anywhere under `contrib`. Should the "multiarch" cron job be nuked?

> Should the "multiarch" cron job be nuked?

Sorry for the confusion, this is only for the `v4.1.1-rhel` branch.  I'll update the PR title.
To be fully clear:  The `multiatch` cirrus-cron job only runs against `main` (as designed).  That's why nuking all this stuff here is perfectly safe.
LGTM but of course CI is broken. If my #16355 is approved and merged, I will cherry-pick it to this branch. If it is not approved, I will backport the whole safe-image fix to this branch.
Since the failures have nothing to do with this PR, I am going to force merge.
